compiled_state.title: Skip RED-marked tests in CI via suite-engine exclusion filter
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context

CI currently fails on every push because 3 RED-marked tests (TDD placeholders for incomplete v2→v3 migration work) cause the Script Tests job to exit non-zero. These are known false-positives that don't impact production — the pre-commit test gate already tolerates them via `.test-index` markers. But `suite-engine.sh` has no exclusion mechanism, so CI reports failure, masking real regressions and eroding trust in the build signal.

## Success Criteria

- `suite-engine.sh` accepts an optional `--exclude-file <path>` flag; tests listed in that file are skipped with a `SKIP (excluded)` status line
- When `--exclude-file` is not passed, suite-engine behavior is identical to today (no behavioral change for existing callers)
- CI generates the exclusion list from `.test-index` RED markers before invoking test runners, so RED-marked tests never run in CI
- CI Script Tests job passes when the only "failures" are RED-marked tests
- Skipped tests are counted and reported in the suite summary (e.g., `PASSED: 178  FAILED: 0  SKIPPED: 3`) so their existence remains visible

## Dependencies

None. RED markers already exist in `.test-index` from the v2→v3 migration (epic d775-4a36, in progress). This epic consumes existing markers; it does not require d775-4a36 to complete first. When d775-4a36 eventually removes the RED markers, the exclusion list becomes empty and CI behavior is unchanged (no tests skipped).

## Approach

Add an `--exclude-file` flag to `suite-engine.sh` that accepts a file of test paths to skip. CI workflow generates this list by parsing `.test-index` for RED markers (`[marker]` entries) before invoking test runners. Backward-compatible: flag is optional, behavior unchanged when omitted.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Revised Approach (after plan review)

File-level exclusion is WRONG. RED markers identify a function boundary within a test file, not whole files to skip. Some RED-marked files have GREEN tests before the marker that must still run.

Revised approach: Extract the RED marker tolerance logic from `record-test-status.sh` (lines 119-496) into a shared library. Suite-engine calls this after a test fails to check if all failures are in the RED zone. If yes → TOLERATED (not counted as failure). If no → FAIL as normal.

Key functions to extract: `get_red_zone_line_number()`, `parse_failing_tests_from_output()`, `get_test_line_number()`, plus the tolerance decision logic.

CI sets `SUITE_RED_TOLERANCE=1` (or provides `.test-index` path) to enable RED marker tolerance in suite-engine. When unset, behavior is identical to today.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Updated Success Criteria (aligned with Revised Approach)

The original SC referenced an --exclude-file flag approach that was superseded by the revised approach in comment 2. Updated criteria to match the implemented RED-zone tolerance mechanism:

- ✅ suite-engine.sh supports SUITE_TEST_INDEX env var; when set, RED-zone-only failures are marked TOLERATED (not counted as failures)
- ✅ When SUITE_TEST_INDEX is not set, suite-engine behavior is identical to before (no behavioral change)
- ✅ CI sets SUITE_TEST_INDEX to .test-index for Hook Tests and Script Tests jobs, enabling RED-zone tolerance
- ✅ CI Script Tests job passes when the only failures are RED-zone marked tests
- ✅ Tolerated tests are counted in summary (PASSED: N  FAILED: N  TOLERATED: N) so their existence remains visible
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Epic complete: all 4 tasks closed, validation score 5/5. RED-zone tolerance implemented and wired to CI.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
